atmosphere.mood: Loud, martial, and hierarchical. Nobody forgets the keep looming outside.
atmosphere.lighting: Well-lit — a great hearth and iron candelabras. The lord's colors on every banner.
atmosphere.smells: Roasting meat, dark ale, leather armor oil, and torch smoke
atmosphere.sounds: Soldiers' laughter, boots on stone, dice on wood, and the occasional clash of steel from the yard outside

layout: A well-built stone-and-timber tavern standing in the shadow of the lord's keep. A large hearth dominates the far wall. Long tables seat rows of soldiers between duties. Banners of the local lord hang from the rafters. A proper bar lines one wall with barrels racked neatly behind.

drinks.name: Dark ale
drinks.description: Strong and bitter — a soldier's drink

drinks.name: Strong wine
drinks.description: Rough but warming, poured from a clay jug

drinks.name: Spiced ale
drinks.description: Winter-brewed, thick with cinnamon and clove

drinks.name: Tournament ale
drinks.description: A special heavy brew served during garrison celebrations — thick and ceremonious

drinks.name: Lord's wine
drinks.description: A decent vintage supplied from the lord's cellars to his favored soldiers and guests

drinks.name: Garrison spirit
drinks.description: Raw spirits distilled on the keep grounds — fiercely strong, barely palatable

drinks.name: Water
drinks.description: A cup of well water, clear and cold

food.name: Roast beef
food.description: Thick slabs off the haunch, served with bread to mop the gravy

food.name: Soldier's pottage
food.description: Hearty lentil and barley stew with smoked pork

food.name: Hard bread
food.description: Dense rye loaf, good for soaking and good for throwing

food.name: Roast boar
food.description: A generous portion of boar from the lord's hunt — crackling skin, fatty and triumphant

food.name: Barracks porridge
food.description: A thick, fortifying porridge served in bulk to soldiers before the morning muster
